verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a single-pass transmembrane protein of the Ig-like cell adhesion / IZUMO family expressed specifically on sperm cells, where it resides on the outer leaflet of the sperm plasma membrane and mediates sperm-oocyte fusion. It has its own TM anchor and is a bona fide surface protein, but its expression is restricted to the germline / gamete lineage (sperm-specific). Per the triage rules, surface display via one's own TM anchor that is restricted to a narrow germline/reproductive lineage maps to contextual / tissue_restricted_surface rather than yes.
